---
title: "[PROJECT TITLE]"
subtitle: "Survey Experiment Design"
author:
  - "Daniel Kuhlen"
  - "Giovanna Lapresa"
  - "Jorge Zavala"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    slide-number: true
    progress: true
    toc: false
    logo: ""
    footer: "Field Experiments Class 2026"
    highlight-style: github
    code-fold: true
    self-contained: true
bibliography: references.bib
---

## Overview

1. Research question & motivation
2. Key design elements
3. Implementation plan
4. Open questions for the group

---

# 1. Question & Motivation

## Research Question

> **[STATE YOUR RESEARCH QUESTION HERE]**

- [Sentence connecting to the broader theoretical literature]
- [Why does this question matter empirically?]
- [What gap does it fill?]

## Theoretical Foundation

- **Theory:** [Name the theory / mechanism]
- **Expectation:** [What does theory predict?]
- **Contribution:** [How does this study advance that theory?]

## Connection to Broader Research Agenda

- [How does this study fit within your larger body of work?]
- [What follow-up questions does it open?]
- [Potential for cumulative / collaborative research?]

---

# 2. Key Design Elements

## Population of Interest

| Dimension | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| Target population | [e.g., adult citizens, party members, legislators] |
| Sampling frame | [e.g., online panel, registry, quota sample] |
| Expected N | [Approximate sample size] |
| Rationale | [Why this population?] |

## Inquiry: Measurement or Causal?

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Primary inquiry**

[Measurement / Causal effect estimation]
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rationale**

[Why this type of inquiry is appropriate for the question]
:::
:::

- **Estimand:** [Define the quantity of interest precisely]
- **Identification strategy:** [How will you achieve identification?]

## Assignment Scheme

```
[TREATMENT ARM A]  ←─────────────────────────────────
                                                      |
Respondents ──── Random assignment ──────────────────|
                                                      |
[TREATMENT ARM B]  ←─────────────────────────────────
                    (+ Control if applicable)
```

- **Unit of randomization:** [Individual / cluster / block]
- **Number of arms:** [e.g., 2×2 factorial, single treatment]
- **Blocking variables:** [e.g., gender, region, partisanship]

## Analysis Plan

- **Primary estimator:** [OLS / ATE / LATE / Difference-in-means]
- **Outcome variable(s):** [List outcomes]
- **Covariates / controls:** [Pre-registered controls]
- **Heterogeneous effects:** [Subgroup analyses planned]
- **Pre-registration:** [OSF / AsPredicted — planned/done]

---

# 3. Implementation

## Team

| Role | Person | Responsibility |
|------|--------|----------------|
| PI | Daniel Kuhlen | Overall design & analysis |
| [Role] | [Name] | [Task] |
| [Role] | [Name] | [Task] |

## Survey Instrument

- **Platform:** Custom HTML/JS (self-hosted)
- **Number of questions:** [N] questions
- **Question types:** [e.g., Likert scales, open-ended, vignettes]
- **Treatment delivery:** [How/where the treatment appears in the flow]

## Timing

| Module | Estimated time |
|--------|---------------|
| Consent & screener | ~1 min |
| [Block 1] | ~[X] min |
| Treatment vignette | ~[X] min |
| [Block 2 — outcomes] | ~[X] min |
| Debriefing | ~1 min |
| **Total** | **~[X] min** |

## Randomization

- **Method:** [Script-based / platform / Qualtrics flow logic]
- **Implementation:** `survey/js/randomization.js`
- **Assignment logging:** [How treatment assignment is stored]
- **Balance checks:** [Planned covariates for balance tables]

## Risks of Interference

- **Spillover:** [Is cross-contamination between units possible?]
- **Attrition:** [Expected dropout and differential attrition risk]
- **Demand effects:** [Steps taken to reduce social desirability bias]
- **Mitigation:** [What measures address these risks?]

## Ethical Considerations

- **Deception:** [Yes/No — if yes, describe and justify debriefing]
- **Sensitive topics:** [Describe any sensitive content]
- **Data privacy:** [Anonymization, GDPR / IRB compliance]
- **IRB status:** [Pending / Approved — institution]

---

# 4. Open Questions

## Questions for the Group

1. [Question about external validity / sampling]
2. [Question about the treatment wording / realism]
3. [Question about the identification strategy]
4. [Question about ethical or practical feasibility]

---

## Thank You

**Contact:** [your.email@university.edu]

*Slides prepared with [Quarto](https://quarto.org)*
